experiment: fp32_residual=True vs False, iter_0 topk tie-margin
setup: Ref iter_0 weights loaded; single sample (8192 tokens) forward; measure top-8 vs top-9 score margin distribution
conclusion: fp32 residual stream does NOT widen the topk tie-margin at iter_0. fraction in bf16 ambiguity is 98.89% either way — untrained router produces structurally narrow score distribution (sigmoid of logits ~N(0, 0.14) is 0.5 ± 0.04) regardless of input precision.
This ONLY rules out fp32_residual as a mitigation for the topk-boundary numerical divergence. It does NOT prove the nano-vs-ref +0.005 nat gap is unmitigable.
overclaim_correction_20260423: Earlier commit 50a3551 and 6bc101d claimed +0.005 is 'the bf16 × 144-expert × K=8 physical floor'. That claim is NOT SUPPORTED by the evidence. I showed that nano and ref must diverge from iter_1, but the FLOOR VALUE of that divergence was never measured. v6-vs-v10 (both nano, different code, same seed) diff is ~0.0001 — 50× smaller than the nano-vs-ref 0.005 — which suggests the 0.005 likely contains a systematic component larger than pure bf16 noise. To establish the actual floor, need nano-vs-nano with different seeds; that experiment has not been run. Downgrade this report's conclusion to 'fp32_residual rejected; floor unmeasured'.
